title: FMT1 stripping logic in verify() is not unit-tested at the Rust level
description: The FMT1 modifier stripping in EccKey::verify is the most subtle logic in the crate (non-trivial bit arithmetic with a nuanced condition on bit 7 + bits 15:12), but it is only tested indirectly via the hardware-only sign_verify_roundtrip integration test. The stripping logic does not depend on a live TPM and can be unit-tested by calling the match logic on carefully chosen synthetic rc values (0x009B, 0x1DB for sig+param1, 0x101 for VER1 code). Rewrite: extract the FMT1 stripping into a small pure function and add a #[test] for each case.
